text: Large language models (LLMs) are a type of artificial intelligence that can understand and generate human-like text. They work by analyzing vast amounts of written data, such as books, articles, and websites. This training data helps the model learn patterns in language, like grammar, facts, and even reasoning. When you give an LLM a prompt, it predicts the most likely next words based on what it has learned. This process is called prompt engineering, where careful wording guides the model to produce useful answers. LLMs can write essays, answer questions, or translate languages. However, they have limits. They may give incorrect or biased information, lack real understanding, and struggle with very new topics not in their training data.
